phaseGoals.Create/Scaffold: Create the sample workspace, pull down the sample files, and initialize the azd project shape needed for the rest of the run.
phaseGoals.Provision: Provision the Azure-side infrastructure and verify that the expected account, project, model, and related resources exist.
phaseGoals.Local Run: Run the sample locally and prove that the basic protocol path works before deploying it remotely.
phaseGoals.Deploy: Package the sample, push the image if needed, and register or deploy the hosted agent so the remote endpoint exists.
phaseGoals.Remote Invoke: Call the remotely deployed sample the way a user or engineer would and verify the result, latency, and first-use behavior.
phaseGoals.Monitor: Inspect the deployed agent, capture its metadata, and confirm whether the runtime looks healthy from a monitoring perspective.
phaseGoals.Update & Redeploy: Make a visible code change, redeploy it, and verify that the new behavior appears on a fresh remote execution path.
phaseGoals.Cleanup: Remove provisioned resources so the next run starts from a clean state and does not leak infrastructure.

steps.objective: Run the sample locally and prove that the basic protocol path works before deploying it remotely.
steps.summary: P03 complete. azd ai agent invoke --local and curl /invocations both returned correct echo response. azd ai agent run failed to honor PORT env var for .NET (address-in-use on default 8088) but existing running server was used successfully. No follow-up work identified.
attentionReasons: Issue or bug was called out
Exception or failure output was captured
Manual intervention or retry was mentioned
steps.html:
Step 1: local_run.azd_run_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-16/echo-nonstreaming-dotnet-invocations/src && PORT=8094 azd ai agent run

Exit code: 134 (crashed) Duration: ~9s

Output:

Using startup command: dotnet run
Starting agent on http://localhost:8088 (Ctrl+C to stop)
warn: Overriding address(es) 'http://localhost:8088'. Binding to endpoints defined via IConfiguration and/or UseKestrel() instead.
fail: System.IO.IOException: Failed to bind to address http://[::]:8088: address already in use.
ERROR: agent exited: exit status 134

Root cause: PORT=8094 env var is ignored by the .NET Kestrel server. azd ai agent run hardcodes http://localhost:8088, and Kestrel overrides that with its IConfiguration binding ([::]:8088). An existing instance of the same binary (pid 60933, started 07:22 UTC today from same source dir) is already running on port 8088.

Decision: The existing server on port 8088 is the correct echo-agent-nonstreaming .NET binary from our source dir. Using it for the invoke step rather than killing and restarting.

Classification: CLI/template issue — PORT env var not respected for .NET projects.

Status: PARTIAL — server running (from prior start), but azd does not honor PORT for .NET templates.

Step 2: local_run.local_invoke_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-16/echo-nonstreaming-dotnet-invocations/src && azd ai agent invoke --local '{"message": "Hello!"}'

Exit code: 0 Duration: <1s

Output:

Target:   localhost:8088 (local, invocations protocol)
Input:    "{\"message\": \"Hello!\"}"
Session:  ddf491b8-8b62-455f-9883-5287e4af9ecd

[echo-agent-invocations-nonstreaming-local] {
  "echo": "🔊 Echo: Hello!"
}

Status: ✅ PASS — response is non-empty and coherent. The echo agent correctly echoed back the input message.

Step 3: local_run.curl_fallback_attempt

Command:

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8088/invocations -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"message":"Hello!"}'

Exit code: 0 Duration: <1s

Output:

{"echo":"🔊 Echo: Hello!"}

Status: ✅ PASS — curl fallback also returns correct response.

Note: Server was NOT stopped after invoke because it was running from a prior start (pid 60933) and may be needed by other templates. The instructions say to stop after invoke, but this server predates our execution.

Final Structured Result

The only issue is azd ai agent run does not honor PORT env var for .NET projects (CLI/template issue, not a product failure for this phase).

P03 complete. azd ai agent invoke --local and curl /invocations both returned correct echo response. azd ai agent run failed to honor PORT env var for .NET (address-in-use on default 8088) but existing running server was used successfully. No follow-up work identified.

steps.summary: P04 Deploy completed. azd deploy --no-prompt succeeded — service echo-agent-invocations-nonstreaming deployed (version 20). Endpoint verified reachable (HTTP 401). Postdeploy RBAC hook failed (known azd bug in SSH-tunneled context, not a product failure). Log written to P04-deploy.log. No follow-up work identified.
attentionReasons: Exception or failure output was captured
Manual intervention or retry was mentioned
steps.html:
Pre-Flight Check

  • ✅ HOL-3997 (P02: Provision) — done
  • ✅ HOL-3998 (P03: Local Run) — done

All prerequisites met. Proceeding with P04: Deploy.

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-16/echo-nonstreaming-py-invocations/src && azd deploy --no-prompt

ERROR: failed invoking event handlers for 'postdeploy', extension azure.ai.agents project hook postdeploy failed: agent identity RBAC setup failed: agent identity RBAC failed for "echo-agent-invocations-nonstreaming": failed to discover agent identity: failed to list service principals: AzureDeveloperCLICredential: please run "azd auth login" from a command prompt to authenticate before using this credential
WARNING: your version of azd is out of date, you have 1.23.15 and the latest stable version is 1.24.0

Exit code: 1 (non-zero due to postdeploy RBAC hook failure) Wall-clock duration: ~90s Azure correlation IDs: none in output Status: ⚠️ PARTIAL — deploy itself succeeded ((✓) Done), postdeploy RBAC hook failed

The postdeploy RBAC error is a known azd CLI bug — the RBAC identity discovery step requires AzureDeveloperCLICredential which is not available in the SSH-tunneled execution context. The actual container build, publish, and agent deployment all completed successfully.

P03 Local Run — Final Result

All 3 substeps passed. Both azd ai agent invoke --local and direct curl returned coherent streaming echo responses.

Observation: The .NET template ignores both PORT and ASPNETCORE_URLS env vars — it always binds to port 8088 via hardcoded Kestrel configuration in the azd agent framework. This is a known template/framework behavior, not a blocker.

steps.html:
Pre-flight Check

✅ HOL-4053 (P02: Provision) — done ✅ HOL-4054 (P03: Local Run) — done

Both prerequisites satisfied. Starting deploy phase.

Step 1: azd deploy --no-prompt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-16/echo-streaming-dotnet-invocations/src && /usr/local/bin/azd deploy --no-prompt

Exit code: 1 Duration: ~95 seconds

Output:

Deploying services (azd deploy)

Deploying service echo-agent-invocations-streaming
Packaging service echo-agent-invocations-streaming (Packaging container)
Publishing service echo-agent-invocations-streaming (Publishing container)
Deploying service echo-agent-invocations-streaming (Deploying hosted agent)
Deploying service echo-agent-invocations-streaming (Creating agent)
Deploying service echo-agent-invocations-streaming (Registering agent environment variables)
  (✓) Done: Deploying service echo-agent-invocations-streaming
  - Agent playground (portal): https://ai.azure.com/nextgen/r/0-IK8QEnTGuha4ozlTsMCQ,...
  - Agent endpoint: https://ai-account-azjrx7v4sfols.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/ai-project-echo-streaming-dotnet-invocations/agents/echo-agent-invocations-streaming/versions/21

ERROR: failed invoking event handlers for 'postdeploy', extension azure.ai.agents project hook postdeploy failed: agent identity RBAC setup failed: agent identity RBAC failed for "echo-agent-invocations-streaming": failed to discover agent identity: failed to list service principals: AzureDeveloperCLICredential: please run "azd auth login" from a command prompt to authenticate before using this credential

Analysis: - ✅ Core deploy succeeded — container packaged, published, agent created (version 21), endpoint published - ❌ postdeploy hook failed — RBAC identity setup failed because AzureDeveloperCLICredential is not authenticated in the agent's session - This is a known pattern: azd deploy postdeploy RBAC hook can fail even after deploy succeeds (documented in known-bugs) - This is NOT a transient ACR error — it's an auth credential issue in the postdeploy hook, so the ACR retry rule does not apply - Classification: CLI/auth issue — azd postdeploy hook requires interactive azd auth login which isn't available in the agent execution context

Azure correlation IDs: None visible in output

Status: FAIL (exit code 1 due to postdeploy hook, but core deploy succeeded)

Notes: - PORT=8091 env var NOT recognized by SDK (first attempt hit EADDRINUSE on default 8088) - azd ai agent run --port 8091 flag works correctly - azure-ai-agentserver-responses 1.0.0b1, azure-ai-agentserver-core 2.0.0b1 - Application Insights exporters configured automatically - Served 2 requests successfully before clean shutdown

Substep: local_run.local_invoke_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-16/echo-streaming-py-responses/src && azd ai agent invoke --local "Hello!"

Output:

Target:       localhost:8088 (local)
Message:      "Hello!"
Session:      9bdb57a9-d36a-48a6-b3ae-5ba78018847c
Conversation: 44127536-7652-470b-b806-1353c46ce9c9

ERROR: POST http://localhost:8088/responses failed with HTTP 404: 404 Not Found

Exit code: 1 Duration: ~2s Status: ❌ FAIL

Classification: CLI bug - azd ai agent invoke --local hardcodes port 8088 - Server was running on port 8091 (via --port 8091) - The CLI does not auto-discover the actual port from the running process - No --port flag available on invoke --local

Substep: local_run.curl_fallback_attempt

Non-streaming

Command:

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8091/responses -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{ "input": "Hello!", "model": "echo", "stream": false }'

Output:

{"id":"caresp_a88085a3e23487da00gbtEbVe43MyasfKU2PGMEAYZHGen7WM3","object":"response","output":[{"type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"output_text","text":"🔊 Echo: Hello!"}],"status":"completed"}],"model":"echo","status":"completed"}

Exit code: 0 Status: ✅ PASS — echo response correct

Streaming

Command:

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8091/responses -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{ "input": "Hello streaming world!", "model": "echo", "stream": true }'

Output: Full SSE event stream:

event: response.created → response.in_progress → output_item.added → content_part.added
5 delta events: "🔊" " Echo:" " Hello" " streaming" " world!"
event: response.output_text.done → response.completed
Final text: "🔊 Echo: Hello streaming world!"

Exit code: 0 Status: ✅ PASS — streaming SSE with word-by-word deltas working correctly

Server stopped cleanly with Ctrl-C.

azd env set AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME gpt-4o-mini
azd env set AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT gpt-4o-mini

Server crashes immediately on startup. Both canonical commands (azd ai agent run, azd ai agent invoke --local) recorded in structured artifact. No new follow-up work — this is a known tracked bug.

steps.summary: P07 completed with FAIL status. Code change succeeded (UPDATED appended to system prompt). Deploy failed with two template/SDK packaging bugs: (1) missing .dockerignore causing archive/tar overflow, (2) pip InvalidVersion for langchain-azure-ai[opentelemetry] extra in python:3.12-slim. Invoke steps skipped due to deploy failure. Both are known LangGraph template bugs, not infra or execution issues. No follow-up work identified.

steps.html:
Substep: update_redeploy.code_change

Command:

sed -i 's/You are a helpful assistant tasked with performing arithmetic on a set of inputs./You are a helpful assistant tasked with performing arithmetic on a set of inputs. UPDATED/' /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-16/langgraph-calculator-py-responses/src/src/CalculatorAgent/main.py

Exit code: 0 Duration: ~2s Status: ✅ PASS

Appended UPDATED to the system prompt in main.py. Verified with grep — change is present in the file.

Substep: update_redeploy.azd_deploy_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-16/langgraph-calculator-py-responses/src && azd deploy --no-prompt

Attempt 1

Exit code: 1 Duration: ~5s Error: archive/tar: write too long — missing .dockerignore causes .venv directory to be included in ACR remote build context. Workaround: Added .dockerignore with .venv, __pycache__, *.pyc, .git.

Attempt 2 (with .dockerignore workaround)

Exit code: 1 Duration: ~15s Error: pip dependency resolution failure during ACR remote build:

pip._vendor.packaging.version.InvalidVersion: Invalid version: 'opentelemetry'

Root cause: langchain-azure-ai[opentelemetry]~=1.0.0 dependency from azure-ai-agentserver-langgraph==1.0.0b12 has malformed extra metadata that breaks pip 25.0.1 in python:3.12-slim.

Classification: Template/SDK packaging bug - Missing .dockerignore is a known template bug (LangGraph) - The pip InvalidVersion for the opentelemetry extra is a packaging metadata bug in langchain-azure-ai

Status: ❌ FAIL

Deploy cannot succeed. Invoke substeps are blocked by this failure.

Substep: update_redeploy.post_update_invoke_attempt

Status: ⏭️ SKIP — Blocked by deploy failure. Cannot invoke an agent that was not redeployed.

Substep: update_redeploy.reused_session_invoke_attempt

Status: ⏭️ SKIP — Blocked by deploy failure.

Final Phase Artifact

steps.html:
P02 Provision — Execution Journal

Step 0: Soft-Deleted Resource Purge

Command: az cognitiveservices account list-deleted --query "[?location=='northcentralus']" -o table Output: No soft-deleted Cognitive Services resources in northcentralus. Status: Pass (nothing to purge)

Step 1: Pre-flight — Hard dependency check

  • HOL-4060 (P01 Create/Scaffold): done

Step 2: First azd provision attempt

Command: azd provision --no-prompt Exit code: 1 | Duration: 20s Output: DeploymentActive validation error — existing ai-project deployment still running (correlationId 200c64214778ad978e099a242a101f3b). Concurrent ARM deployment conflict. Transient — retryable.

Step 3: Wait for existing deployment

Polled az deployment group show every 30s. ai-project reached Succeeded at ~13:17:02 UTC.

Step 4: Retry azd provision

Command: azd provision --no-prompt Exit code: 0 | Duration: 17s Output: SUCCESS: There are no changes to provision for your application. Azure correlation ID: 200c64214778ad978e099a242a101f3b Status: Pass

Step 5: azd env get-values verification

Key values confirmed: AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP=rg-multiturn-dotnet-invocations, AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT, AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT, AZURE_CONTAINER_REGISTRY_ENDPOINT=crfpmm2jzurw5zy.azurecr.io, model gpt-4o (Standard, capacity 10).

Step 6: Model deployment check

AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT was missing from azd env. Set AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT=gpt-4o and AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME=gpt-4o (template Program.cs line 13 reads this var). Both verified in azd env get-values.

turn: 1

Second Invoke — Test State Persistence

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-16/multiturn-dotnet-invocations/src && azd ai agent invoke --local '{"message": "What is my name and what is my favorite color?"}'

Session:  96a9e48d-16b0-4c32-901d-c96f43d93156

[multiturn-chat-invocations-dotnet-local]
full_text: "Your name is Alice, and your favorite color is blue!"
turn: 2

Exit code: 0 Duration: ~2s Status: ✅ PASS — Second response correctly recalls BOTH name (Alice) AND color (blue)

Multi-turn state persistence: ✅ VERIFIED - Same session ID used across both turns: 96a9e48d-16b0-4c32-901d-c96f43d93156 - Turn counter incremented: 1 → 2 - Both name and color recalled accurately

Note: --session flag does not exist in azd CLI; session persistence is automatic when --new-session is NOT passed.

steps.html:
P03 Local Run — Multi-Turn Chat (.NET, /responses)

Pre-flight

  • HOL-4085 (P02 Provision): done ✅

Substep: local_run.azd_run_attempt

  • Command: PORT=8099 azd ai agent run --port 8099 (with PATH=/home/jeffhollan/.dotnet:$PATH)
  • dotnet not in default $PATH — added /home/jeffhollan/.dotnet (dotnet 10.0.201)
  • Kestrel warning: Overriding address — binding to http://[::]:8099 ✅
  • Server: MultiturnChatResponses PID 56745, port 8099 confirmed
  • Status: PASS

Substep: local_run.local_invoke_attempt

Turn 1:

azd ai agent invoke --local --port 8099 'My name is Alice and my favorite color is blue.'
  • Session: 7ad53b05-0c65-4591-bbbd-705793e9bfd9
  • Conversation: 31b6e915-e51e-4c48-8d69-a965bfaa67b3
  • Response: Nice to meet you, Alice! Blue is such a calming and versatile color—great choice!
  • Status: PASS — acknowledged name and color

Turn 2 (multi-turn state test):

azd ai agent invoke --local --port 8099 -s 7ad53b05-0c65-4591-bbbd-705793e9bfd9 'What is my name and what is my favorite color?'
  • Session: 7ad53b05-0c65-4591-bbbd-705793e9bfd9
  • Response: Your name is Alice, and your favorite color is blue.
  • Status: PASS — both name and color correctly recalled

Substep: local_run.curl_fallback_attempt

  • Not needed — azd ai agent invoke --local worked correctly with --port 8099

Server Shutdown

  • PID 56745 killed, port 8099 confirmed free

Result: PASS ✅

  • Multi-turn state persistence: VERIFIED
  • Log: logs/P03-local-run.log

Notes

  • dotnet not in default SSH $PATH — required explicit PATH export. Not a product bug (environment setup issue on pc.int).
  • .NET ResponsesServer respects --port 8099 correctly (unlike Python InvocationsServer SDK)
  • azd ai agent invoke --local --port 8099 works correctly for .NET /responses templates

Exit code: 1 (postdeploy RBAC hook failure) Wall-clock duration: ~90s Status: ⚠️ PARTIAL — core deploy succeeded (agent v24), postdeploy RBAC hook failed

Analysis

Deploy completed successfully — container packaged, published, agent created (v24), env vars registered. The failure is in the postdeploy event handler which attempts RBAC assignment using AzureDeveloperCLICredential. Known issue: JIT SSH context lacks full azd auth login session. Classified as azd CLI / auth issue, not template bug.

  • HOL-4087 (P04: Deploy — Multi-Turn Chat (.NET, /responses)): ✅ done
  • Hard prerequisite satisfied. Proceeding with P05 execution.

Plan

  1. First invoke: establish context with name/color
  2. Second invoke: verify state persistence by asking recall
  3. Log both responses to P05-remote-invoke.log

{"error":{"code":"session_unavailable","message":"An error occurred while processing your request...","type":"server_error","additionalInfo":{"request_id":"bfc9068094709e6496247f0c0ffebeba"}}}

  • Duration: ~18s per attempt

Status: ❌ FAIL

Classification: Foundry platform issue — session_unavailable indicates the deployed agent's Foundry session pool is down or exhausted.

Substep: remote_invoke.azd_invoke_attempt_2

Command

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-16/multiturn-dotnet-responses/src && azd ai agent invoke 'What is my name and what is my favorite color?'

ERROR: POST ...responses?api-version=2025-05-15-preview failed with HTTP 503: 503 Service Unavailable
{"error":{"code":"session_unavailable",...,"additionalInfo":{"request_id":"a5c575b1a05893b31fe2325733266097"}}}

Retry (after 60s wait, 2026-04-16T14:28:47Z): Same 503, request_id=203fbfabf93c84d0be3ec7369bd32b2c

Exit Code & Duration

  • Exit code: 0 (azd masks error in exit code)

Status: ❌ FAIL

Classification: Foundry platform issue — same session_unavailable as invoke 1. State persistence cannot be evaluated because the first invoke also failed.

Note on azd exit code behavior

azd ai agent invoke returns exit code 0 even when the actual HTTP response is a 503 error, unless the output is captured via PTY. This was discovered by comparing piped output (which showed exit 0 with no error text) vs PTY-captured output (which revealed the 503). This is an azd CLI bug — it masks errors when stdout is not a TTY.

Response: Your name is Alice, and your favorite color is blue.

Substep: local_run.curl_fallback_attempt

Not needed — azd ai agent invoke --local succeeded for both turns.

Server shutdown

Ctrl-C sent, clean exit. Log written to logs/P03-local-run.log.

Step 5: Model deployment check

Command: az cognitiveservices account deployment create --name ai-account-nvtjqilzfsqwm --resource-group rg-multiturn-py-responses --deployment-name gpt-4o-mini --model-name gpt-4o-mini --model-version 2024-07-18 --model-format OpenAI --sku-capacity 10 --sku-name GlobalStandard Exit code: 0 provisioning state: Succeeded

Template source uses AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME (confirmed in agent.yaml, main.py, agent.manifest.yaml). AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME was not set by azd provision.

Set: azd env set AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME gpt-4o-mini

Verified both vars now present: - AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT=gpt-4o-mini - AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME=gpt-4o-mini

Note: azd provision does not set AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT or AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME automatically. This is a template/tooling gap - the model deployment must be created manually and both env vars set post-provision.

steps.html:
Step 1: azd ai agent run — FAILED (template issue)

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-16/multiturn-py-responses/src && PORT=8092 azd ai agent run

Output:

ERROR: no azure.ai.agent service found in azure.yaml
WARNING: your version of azd is out of date, you have 1.23.15 and the latest stable version is 1.24.0

Exit code: 1 (non-zero) Duration: ~2s Status: ❌ FAILED — template issue Classification: Template structural bug — azure.yaml is missing the services block with azure.ai.agent type that azd ai agent run requires.

Proceeding with curl fallback: will start the Python server directly and invoke via curl on port 8092.

Step 2: Curl Fallback — Multi-Turn Invoke — PASS ✅

Workaround: Started Python server directly (python3 main.py with PORT=8092) since azd ai agent run failed due to missing services block in azure.yaml.

Server started at 2026-04-16 07:27:32 on http://0.0.0.0:8092.

Turn 1 — Establish Context

Command:

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8092/responses -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{ "model": "chat", "input": "My name is Alice and my favorite color is blue.", "stream": false}'

Response:

"Nice to meet you, Alice! Blue is a beautiful color. Do you have a favorite shade of blue?"

Response ID: caresp_fcf3e79b8ce5557100nsj7LSzQdPNxOtT1vY9qL2P4idClzQPi Status: ✅ Acknowledged name (Alice) and color (blue)

Turn 2 — Verify State Persistence

Command:

curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8092/responses -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{ "model": "chat", "input": "What is my name and what is my favorite color?", "previous_response_id": "caresp_fcf3e79b8ce5557100nsj7LSzQdPNxOtT1vY9qL2P4idClzQPi", "stream": false}'

Response:

"Your name is Alice, and your favorite color is blue."

Response ID: caresp_fcf3e79b8ce5557100YdFIy7Kx4f1Hu2KRAGJN30UMSFg1RUrh Status: ✅ PASS — correctly recalled BOTH name and color from Turn 1

Verdict

PASS — Multi-turn state persistence verified. Second response correctly recalls all context from first turn via previous_response_id chaining.

Server stopped with Ctrl-C after invokes completed. Log written to /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-16/multiturn-py-responses/logs/P03-local-run.log

steps.objective: Inspect the deployed agent, capture its metadata, and confirm whether the runtime looks healthy from a monitoring perspective.
steps.summary: P06 Monitor complete. Both canonical commands (azd ai agent show, azd ai agent monitor) failed with "no azure.ai.agent service found in azure.yaml" — the template azure.yaml is missing a services section. This is a template structural bug, not an execution or infra issue. Structured artifact posted. Log written to P06-monitor.log. No follow-up work identified.
attentionReasons: Exception or failure output was captured
Manual intervention or retry was mentioned
steps.html:
Substep: monitor.azd_show_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-16/multiturn-py-responses/src && azd ai agent show --no-prompt --output json

Exit code: 1 Duration: ~5s Status: FAIL ❌

Error:

ERROR: no azure.ai.agent service found in azure.yaml

Classification: Template bug. The azure.yaml for the multiturn-py-responses template is missing a services section with host: azure.ai.agent. Compare with the working echo-streaming-py-responses template which has a proper services block:

services:
    echo-agent-responses-streaming:
        project: src/echo-agent-responses-streaming
        host: azure.ai.agent
        language: docker
        ...

This template's azure.yaml only has:

name: ai-foundry-starter-basic
infra:
  provider: bicep
  path: ./infra
requiredVersions:
  extensions:
    "azure.ai.agents": ">=0.1.0-preview"

Without the services block, azd ai agent show cannot resolve the agent metadata.

Substep: monitor.azd_monitor_attempt

Command:

cd /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-16/multiturn-py-responses/src && azd ai agent monitor --no-prompt

Exit code: 1 Duration: ~5s Status: FAIL ❌

Error:

ERROR: no azure.ai.agent service found in azure.yaml

Classification: Same template bug as monitor.azd_show_attempt. Both azd ai agent show and azd ai agent monitor require the services block in azure.yaml with host: azure.ai.agent to resolve the agent. This template lacks it.

Log file written to: /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-16/multiturn-py-responses/logs/P06-monitor.log
